design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team composition, execute passing and shooting mechanics, defend against opponent team, score goals to win matches
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time competitive sport simulation with asymmetric team roster customization affecting playstyle matchups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and control an ice hockey team with tactical roster selection impacting gameplay strategy and execution
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Roster building depth unusual for 1988 sports games—player customization (weight, speed, strength attributes per player) directly affects match outcomes and encourages team composition experimentation rather than generic sport simulation

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can customize team roster (player types, attributes) before matches to match difficulty; hidden 'secret tricks' (removable goalies, friction removal) allow further difficulty adjustment

progression.upgrade_system: Roster customization system allowing player selection and attribute configuration per team slot
progression.unlock_mechanism: Team selection and hidden modifier mechanics (A+B button combinations on title/team select screens reveal goalie removal and friction modifiers)
replayability_hooks: Multiple team rosters with distinct player types and regional variants (FDS vs NES team differences)
Hidden secret mechanics discoverable through button combinations
Asymmetric team composition strategy encouraging varied playstyle experimentation
Regional versions feature different team lineups and music, providing variant experiences

world_building_techniques: Team selection screen establishes national/regional identity through team names and player type silhouettes
Regional differences (Japan→Sweden swap in NES version) implicitly reflect localization approach
Traditional hockey victory jingle (NES version) anchors sport authenticity

faction_indicators: Team roster selection creates implicit affiliation identity
Player type selection (weight/speed/strength archetypes) signals strategic playstyle commitment
danger_signals: Hidden 'secret tricks' enable goalkeepers removal (zero defense possibility), friction elimination (infinite puck momentum)—exploitable metagame mechanics
Roster imbalance potential through attribute stacking on limited player slots
environmental_storytelling: Team lineups reflect regional locales (USA, Sweden, Canada, etc.) in selection screen
Player type icons communicate role archetypes visually without text

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor Ice Hockey NES/FDS article)
